page: Microsoft Azure M128s is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instruction sets. The server is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core, and includes 4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively, with poor single-threaded compression and decompression rates. Conversely, multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, reaching top-tier status in the top 10% for uncached large-block reads and writes at 420.25 GB/sec and 186.91 GB/sec respectively. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25%. LLM inference speeds are average for prompt processing but poor for text generation.

Given its massive memory footprint and high vCPU count, the M128s represents a high-resource density profile. While single-threaded tasks are inefficient on this hardware, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong multi-core throughput make it highly cost-effective for large-scale, memory-bound workloads. It is well-suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise resource planning applications, and heavy multi-threaded data processing tasks that require substantial RAM.
description: Microsoft Azure M128s is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 128 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores. It features 2048.0 GB of system memory and 4194 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. While single-core performance is weak, the instance delivers strong multi-core capabilities and top-tier uncached memory bandwidth, reaching 420.25 GB/sec for reads. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and heavy multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M128s is a memory-optimized VM featuring 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and 4194 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M128s is a memory-optimized VM with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and 4194 GB local storage, built for large databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity, strong multi-core throughput, and high-speed uncached memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8280M processors
2048.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per core
4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier uncached memory read bandwidth of 420.25 GB/sec
Strong multi-core performance and high Redis SET throughput
Designed for large-scale databases and in-memory caching
